nl_question: Who is the current president of the United States?
category_description: A question entirely outside the biomedical domain. Tests whether the LLM correctly defers to its scope check rather than reflexively answering any question its training data covers.
rationale: This is the canonical 'is the LLM staying in scope?' probe. It surfaces a specific failure mode of LLM-backed retrieval systems: the LLM bypasses the retrieval system entirely and answers from training data. For PloverAI, every legitimate answer must trace back to an edge in RTX-KG2.10.2c — an answer about a US president cannot. The system observed this failure during early testing (see pipeline/benchmark/known_issues.md); this question is a regression test for that bug.

expected_scope_check.reason_pattern: Stage -1 should return a one-sentence reason identifying this as politics / government / general world knowledge.

example_acceptable_reasons: Questions about politics or current heads of state are not biomedical-KG queries.
This is a general-knowledge / political question rather than a biomedical-relationship question.
Asking who is the current US president is outside the biomedical knowledge graph's scope.
expected_user_response_template: Fixed body produced by pipeline._format_out_of_scope_explanation. Only the line under "## Reason" is variable.
